manual.title: User Manual
manual.introDesc: A full-stack quantitative trading platform for individual investors. Write strategies, run backtests, monitor paper trading, sync real positions, and run stock pickers — all in one place. Follow the five steps below to get started.

strategy.title: Write a Strategy
strategy.desc: Use the Strategy Workshop to write Python-based quant strategies with the built-in CodeMirror editor and persistent storage.
strategy.item1: Click New Strategy and fill in the name and unique ID
strategy.item2: Write your strategy code based on the akquant framework
strategy.item3: Save and manage your strategies from the list anytime

backtest.title: Run Backtests
backtest.desc: In the Backtest Center, configure parameters for a saved strategy and run historical backtests to validate its performance.
backtest.item1: Select the strategy you want to validate
backtest.item2: Set the symbols, date range, and initial cash
backtest.item3: Run the backtest and review return, Sharpe ratio, and max drawdown

paperTrading.title: Paper Trading Monitor
paperTrading.desc: Connect your strategy to akquant's paper trading mode to observe performance without risking real capital.
paperTrading.item1: Create a new session and bind it to a strategy
paperTrading.item2: Start the session to generate real-time signals and simulated positions
paperTrading.item3: Monitor P&L changes to inform real trading decisions

sync.title: Real Position Sync
sync.desc: Using a dual-ledger design, record your actual broker trades and reconcile them with simulated holdings. Supports auto fee calculation, CSV batch import, and batch sync.
sync.item1: The simulated ledger is maintained automatically by akquant after running a paper trading session
sync.item2: Use one-click sync on the Paper Trading page; fees are auto-calculated based on your account settings
sync.item3: Import CSV settlement statements to bulk-write real trades and automatically update positions
sync.item4: Use batch entry mode to select multiple pending signals and sync them all at once
sync.item5: Open the Daily Report to review position differences, unsynced signals, and real P&L

stockPicker.title: Stock Pickers
stockPicker.desc: Use akshare to fetch A-share market data, write custom screening logic, or use the built-in weekly auto-picker.
stockPicker.item1: Go to the Stock Picker page; a built-in volume-momentum picker is scheduled every Friday
stockPicker.item2: Edit the built-in code or create your own custom picker
stockPicker.item3: Run a picker to generate a stock pool, then create a backtest or import symbols directly
stockPicker.item4: Toggle weekly push reminders in settings; future support for WeChat/email notifications is planned

manual.tip1: It's recommended to validate a strategy via backtest before running paper trading
manual.tip2: Changes to a strategy won't automatically update existing backtests or sessions — recreate them if needed
manual.tip3: Real position sync is designed to scale smoothly to MiniQMT automated trading in the future
manual.tip4: Click Account Settings on the Paper Trading page to customize commission rate, stamp tax, and transfer fee
manual.tip5: When a red badge appears next to Paper Trading in the sidebar, it means there are overdue unsynced signals
manual.tip6: For troubleshooting, check the backend OpenAPI docs at /docs after starting the server
manual.tip7: Weekly picks update every Friday at 15:05; a red badge on Stock Pickers means new picks are available
